name: EV Charger Installation
shortDesc: OZEV-compliant home and workplace EV charge point installation.
intro: As electric vehicles become the norm across Scotland, we design and install home and workplace charging points that are safe, fast, and built to last. Every installation includes a full circuit assessment so your charger runs on a supply that can actually handle it.
highlights: Clean, tidy cable routing and mounting
whyUs: Every install starts with a proper load calculation, not a guess
Smart, app-connected chargers fitted to current regulations
Cable routing planned to look tidy, not just to work
Same electrician from quote through to sign-off
signs: You've bought or leased an EV and need a home charge point fitted
Your workplace wants to offer staff or fleet EV charging
Your existing charger is outdated or isn't smart-enabled
You're a landlord or developer adding charging to a property or development

faqs.q: Do I need planning permission for a home charge point?
faqs.a: Almost never for a standard domestic installation. We'll flag it if your property is an exception, such as a listed building.

faqs.q: How long does installation take?
faqs.a: Most home installs are done within half a day, depending on cable run length and whether any consumer unit work is needed.

faqs.q: What if my consumer unit can't handle a charger?
faqs.a: We check this during the free survey. If an upgrade is needed, we'll quote for it alongside the charger install.

faqs.q: Are smart chargers a legal requirement?
faqs.a: Yes — since 2022, new home and workplace chargers in Great Britain must have smart charging capability by law. All our installs meet this.

faqs.q: Can you install chargers for businesses and fleets?
faqs.a: Yes, including multi-charger workplace setups with load balancing so you're not paying to upgrade your whole supply unnecessarily.

benefit: Charge Your EV at Home, Safely & Fast

deepDive.heading: What Actually Happens During an EV Charger Install
paragraphs: It starts with the free survey, not the fitting — we check your incoming supply, existing consumer unit, and the cable route between the board and where the charger's going, because that's what decides the real cost and timeline, not the charger itself.

On install day, we run the cable, mount the unit, and connect it into your consumer unit — upgrading the board first if the survey flagged it as necessary. Every charger is then load-tested, commissioned, and certified before we hand it over.

Since 2022, every new home and workplace charger in Great Britain is legally required to have smart charging capability, meaning it can be scheduled and monitored remotely — that's standard on everything we fit, not an optional extra.

concerns.q: Will my existing electrics actually cope with a charger?
concerns.a: That's exactly what the free survey is for — we check your supply and consumer unit before quoting, so there are no surprises once work starts.

concerns.q: How long will the power be off for?
concerns.a: Usually a short isolation while we connect into the consumer unit, not an all-day outage — we'll tell you the expected window before we start.

concerns.q: How long does the whole job take?
concerns.a: Most home installs are done within half a day, start to finish.

name: Home & Business Rewiring
shortDesc: Full and partial rewires for homes, flats, offices and shops.
intro: Old, unsafe, or undersized wiring is one of the most common issues we find on inspection. Whether it's a full rewire of a tenement flat or a partial upgrade in a working office, we plan around your schedule and leave every wall making good.
whyUs: Experience with Glasgow's tenement and period wiring quirks
Chasing, channelling, and making good handled by the same team
Businesses kept running with phased, out-of-hours scheduling
Every rewire tested and certified before we leave
signs: Lights flicker or dim when other appliances switch on
Sockets or switches feel warm, or you smell burning
Fuses trip often, or you still have a fusebox with rewireable fuses
The property hasn't been rewired in 25–30 years
An EICR or a mortgage/insurance survey has flagged the wiring

faqs.q: How long does a full rewire take?
faqs.a: A typical two or three-bedroom tenement flat takes around a week. We'll give you an exact timeline once we've surveyed the property.

faqs.q: Do I need to move out during a rewire?
faqs.a: Not always for a partial rewire, but for a full rewire it's usually easier to have the property empty. We'll talk through the options for your specific job.

faqs.q: Will you make good after chasing walls?
faqs.a: We fill and make good all channels we cut. If you need a full plaster skim afterwards, we can recommend a plasterer we work with regularly.

faqs.q: Can a business rewire be done without closing?
faqs.a: Often yes, by phasing the work area by area or working out of hours — we'll scope this properly at quote stage.

faqs.q: Is the work certified?
faqs.a: Yes, every rewire is tested to BS7671 and certified on completion.

benefit: Safe, Modern Wiring You Can Trust

deepDive.heading: What Does a Full Rewire Actually Involve?
paragraphs: A rewire happens in two stages: first fix, where we run new cabling through walls, floors, and ceilings to every socket, switch, and light position; and second fix, where we connect and terminate everything — sockets, switches, the consumer unit — once walls are closed up again. Between the two, everything is tested to BS7671 before we sign it off.

Glasgow's tenement and period flats bring their own quirks that a generic rewire quote won't account for — lath-and-plaster walls behave differently to modern stud walls when it comes to chasing cable routes, and shared stairwells or factor-managed common areas sometimes need a quick word with the factor before we touch anything communal. We've worked enough of these properties across the city to know which questions to ask before we start, not halfway through.

concerns.q: Do I need a full rewire, or would a partial one do?
concerns.a: It depends on the property — sometimes a targeted partial rewire of the affected circuits is genuinely enough. We'll tell you honestly which one your property needs after a proper look, not just upsell the bigger job.

concerns.q: Is my current wiring actually safe to keep using?
concerns.a: That's what an EICR is for — if you're not sure, we'd rather test it properly than guess. It tells you exactly what's safe, what's borderline, and what needs attention now.

concerns.q: Will the power be off during the work?
concerns.a: Yes, while we connect the new wiring in — we agree the timing with you in advance and keep it to the minimum needed.

concerns.q: How long will it actually take?
concerns.a: A typical two or three-bedroom flat takes around a week. Larger or occupied properties take longer, which we'll scope honestly at survey stage.

commonProblems: Old or outdated wiring that's never been upgraded
Damaged, cracked, or discoloured sockets and switches
Lights tripping when other appliances switch on
Sockets tripping repeatedly, especially in older properties

name: Electrical Inspection & Testing
shortDesc: EICR reports for landlords, homeowners, and commercial properties.
intro: An Electrical Installation Condition Report (EICR) is a legal requirement for rental properties and good practice for every home or business. We test the full installation and give you a clear, honest report — not a sales pitch.
highlights: Fast turnaround for lets and sales
Remedial work quoted on the spot
whyUs: Reports written in plain English, not just code references
Fast turnaround for time-sensitive lets and sales
Remedial work quoted on-site, no waiting for a second visit
Landlord compliance experience across Glasgow lets
signs: You're renewing a tenancy or letting a property for the first time
You're buying or selling and need proof of electrical condition
There's no EICR on record for the property
Your insurer has asked for an up-to-date report
You've just had work done and want it independently checked

faqs.q: How often does a rented property need an EICR in Scotland?
faqs.a: At least every 5 years, or at change of tenancy if the previous report is due to expire — it's a legal requirement under the Repairing Standard.

faqs.q: How long does testing take?
faqs.a: A typical flat or house takes 2–4 hours on site. Larger commercial properties take longer, which we'll scope in advance.

faqs.q: What happens if the property fails?
faqs.a: We list every issue found, rated by severity, and can quote for the remedial work on the spot so you're not left chasing a second contractor.

faqs.q: Do I get the certificate the same day?
faqs.a: You'll get a verbal summary on site, with the full written EICR following shortly after.

faqs.q: Is an EICR a legal requirement for landlords?
faqs.a: Yes, under the Scottish Repairing Standard, private landlords must hold a satisfactory EICR.

deepDive.heading: What We Actually Test During an EICR
paragraphs: An EICR isn't a quick visual once-over — it's a full inspection combining a visual check of accessible wiring and fittings with 'dead testing' (checked with the power off, covering continuity, insulation resistance, and earthing) and 'live testing' (checked with the power on, covering RCD trip times and polarity).

Every issue we find gets a code, not just a pass/fail: C1 means danger present, needing action immediately; C2 means potentially dangerous, needing to be addressed urgently; C3 is an improvement recommended but not a compliance failure; and FI means further investigation is needed. We explain what each one actually means for your property in plain English on the day, not just in a report you get later.

concerns.q: Is my current installation actually safe to keep using?
concerns.a: That's precisely what the test tells you — a clear pass, or a specific list of what needs attention, rated by how urgent it is. No guessing.

concerns.q: How long will the test take?
concerns.a: A typical flat or house takes 2–4 hours on site. Larger commercial properties take longer, which we'll scope in advance.

concerns.q: What if it doesn't pass?
concerns.a: We list every issue found, rated by severity, and can quote for the remedial work on the spot — you're not left chasing a second contractor to fix what we found.

commonProblems: Out of date or missing electrical certificates
No record of when the property was last tested

name: Consumer Unit Upgrades
shortDesc: Modern, RCD-protected consumer units to replace old fuse boards.
intro: Many homes and small businesses across Glasgow are still running on outdated fuse boards with no RCD protection. We replace these with modern, correctly rated consumer units that meet current regulations and give you real protection.
whyUs: Boards sized properly for your actual circuits, not off-the-shelf guesses
RCD and surge protection fitted as standard on new boards
Same-day upgrades in most homes, minimal power-down time
Certified to BS7671 on completion
signs: You still have an old fuse box with rewireable wire fuses
Your board has no RCD (residual current device) protection
The board trips repeatedly or feels warm to the touch
An EICR or mortgage survey has flagged the consumer unit
You're adding an EV charger, extension, or extra circuits and need more capacity

faqs.q: How long does an upgrade take?
faqs.a: Most homes are done in half a day to a full day, depending on how many circuits need re-terminating.

faqs.q: Will the power be off during the work?
faqs.a: Yes, temporarily — we'll agree a time that works for you and keep the power-down window as short as possible.

faqs.q: Do I need a new consumer unit if mine fails an EICR?
faqs.a: If the EICR marks it as a serious risk (C1/C2), yes — we can quote for the upgrade at the same visit.

faqs.q: What size consumer unit do I need?
faqs.a: It depends on your existing circuits and any future additions like an EV charger — we work this out during the free survey.

faqs.q: Is the upgrade certified?
faqs.a: Yes, every consumer unit upgrade is tested and certified on completion.

benefit: A Safer Fuse Board, Sorted Same Day

deepDive.heading: RCDs, RCBOs, and Why Your Board Actually Matters
paragraphs: An old rewireable fuse box protects your wiring from overheating — that's it. It does nothing to protect a person from electric shock, because it has no RCD (residual current device) protection, which is why boards like these are one of the most common issues we flag on an EICR.

A modern consumer unit fits RCDs or RCBOs (a combined breaker and RCD per circuit) as standard, which cut the power in milliseconds if they detect current leaking somewhere it shouldn't — like through a person. We size and split circuits properly across the board so one nuisance trip doesn't take out your whole house, which is the other common complaint we hear about old or badly-specified boards.

concerns.q: Do I need a whole new board, or can mine be repaired?
concerns.a: If it's an old rewireable fuse box with no RCD protection, repairing it isn't really an option — it needs replacing to meet current safety standards. If it's a modern board with one faulty part, sometimes a repair genuinely is enough, and we'll tell you honestly which applies.

concerns.q: Is my current board safe to keep using in the meantime?
concerns.a: We'll tell you straight during the survey — if it's a serious risk we won't let you sit on it, but plenty of ageing boards are fine to use short-term while you plan the upgrade.

concerns.q: Will the power be off during the work?
concerns.a: Yes, temporarily — we agree a time that works for you and keep the power-down window as short as possible.

concerns.q: How long does it actually take?
concerns.a: Most homes are done in half a day to a full day, depending on how many circuits need re-terminating.

commonProblems: Lights tripping when other appliances switch on
Power outages traced back to an old or overloaded board

name: Commercial Installations & Lighting
shortDesc: Electrical fit-outs and lighting for offices, shops, and units.
intro: From new office fit-outs to shop and unit lighting upgrades, we work with property developers and commercial clients to deliver installations that are on spec, on time, and built to withstand daily commercial use.
whyUs: Work scheduled around your trading hours, not the other way round
LED lighting design that cuts running costs, not just bulbs
Comfortable working directly with architects, shopfitters, and developers
Maintenance contracts available once the fit-out is done
signs: You're opening a new unit, office, or shop
Your current lighting is outdated fluorescent tube and costly to run
You're planning a fit-out or refurbishment and need electrical scoped in
You need emergency lighting or exit signage brought up to compliance
You want an ongoing maintenance contract instead of one-off call-outs

faqs.q: Can work be done outside trading hours?
faqs.a: Yes — evenings, overnight, or phased around opening hours is common for retail and office fit-outs.

faqs.q: Do you handle emergency lighting and exit signage?
faqs.a: Yes, including testing and certification to keep you compliant.

faqs.q: Can you work directly with our architect or shopfitter?
faqs.a: Yes, we regularly work from architect drawings and coordinate directly with the wider fit-out team.

faqs.q: Do you offer ongoing maintenance contracts?
faqs.a: Yes, for commercial clients who'd rather have a fixed point of contact than call around for every issue.

faqs.q: How long does a typical office fit-out take?
faqs.a: It depends on scale — we'll give you a firm timeline once we've seen the drawings or site.

deepDive.heading: Planning Electrical Work Around a Live Business
paragraphs: The biggest cost in a commercial job is rarely the materials — it's downtime. We scope every fit-out around your actual trading hours from the start, whether that means working evenings, overnight, or phasing the job area by area so the rest of the unit stays open.

LED retrofits are usually the quickest win we do for existing commercial spaces — swapping outdated fluorescent tube fittings for LED cuts running costs and reduces how often you're replacing failed tubes, and it's often done in phases without shutting anything down.

concerns.q: Will this disrupt trading?
concerns.a: Not if we plan it properly — evenings, overnight, or phasing area by area is common, and we'll build the schedule around your hours, not the other way round.

concerns.q: Does the power need to be isolated during the work?
concerns.a: For parts of it, yes — we plan isolations for the smallest area and shortest time possible, and agree the timing with you in advance.

concerns.q: How long will it take?
concerns.a: It depends on scale — we'll give you a firm timeline once we've seen the drawings or site, not a vague estimate.

name: Domestic Extensions & Conversions
shortDesc: Electrical works for extensions, conversions, and renovations.
intro: Extending or converting your home means your electrics need to extend with it. We work alongside your builder or architect from first fix to final certification, covering everything from extra sockets to new circuits and lighting design.
whyUs: First and second fix scheduled around your builder's timeline
Loft, garage, and outbuilding conversion experience
Direct coordination with your builder or architect
Building Standards certification provided on completion
signs: You're planning an extension or loft conversion
You're converting a garage or outbuilding into living or office space
Your builder has asked for an electrician to quote first and second fix
You need extra circuits for a new kitchen, bathroom, or utility room
You want outdoor sockets or lighting added as part of the build

faqs.q: When should I bring you in on the project?
faqs.a: As early as possible — ideally once first-fix stage is being planned, so sockets, lighting, and circuits are positioned correctly first time.

faqs.q: Do you liaise directly with my builder or architect?
faqs.a: Yes, we're used to working from drawings and coordinating on-site directly with the rest of the build team.

faqs.q: Is Building Standards certification included?
faqs.a: Yes, we certify the electrical work to the standard your local authority requires.

faqs.q: How long does first fix take on a typical extension?
faqs.a: It depends on size and circuit count — we'll confirm a timeline once we've seen the plans.

faqs.q: Can you wire outbuildings and garden rooms too?
faqs.a: Yes, including outdoor supplies, garden offices, and outhouse conversions.

benefit: Electrics Ready Before the Plasterer Arrives

deepDive.heading: First Fix vs Second Fix, Explained
paragraphs: If you've not been through a build before, 'first fix' and 'second fix' get thrown around a lot without much explanation. First fix happens once the structure's up but before walls are closed — we run all the cabling to every socket, switch, and light position, coordinated with the builder so nothing gets boarded over before it's in place.

Second fix comes after plastering and decoration — we come back to actually terminate everything: fitting sockets, switches, light fittings, and connecting into the consumer unit. Getting the timing of both right with your builder is what keeps an extension on schedule, which is why we'd rather be brought in at the planning stage than after first fix should have already happened.

concerns.q: Will the power need to be isolated while you work?
concerns.a: Only for the specific circuits we're connecting into, and usually briefly — the rest of your home stays powered as normal.

concerns.q: How long does first fix take?
concerns.a: It depends on size and circuit count — we'll confirm a timeline once we've seen the plans, in step with your builder's schedule.

name: Kitchen Electrical Installations
shortDesc: Sockets, lighting, and appliance circuits for new and renovated kitchens.
intro: Kitchens carry more electrical load than any other room in the house. We plan sockets, under-unit lighting, and dedicated appliance circuits properly the first time, so your new kitchen works exactly as designed.
highlights: Dedicated circuits for ovens, hobs, and appliances
Socket layout planned with your kitchen fitter
whyUs: Dedicated circuits planned for ovens, hobs, and heavy appliances
Socket and lighting layout agreed with your kitchen fitter before first fix
Under-unit and feature lighting wired in cleanly, no visible cabling
Safe isolation and zoning handled correctly around sinks and wet areas
signs: You're planning a new kitchen or full renovation
You're adding an island that needs new sockets or power
You're upgrading to an induction hob or double oven needing a dedicated circuit
You want under-cabinet or feature lighting
Your current sockets don't suit the new layout

faqs.q: When should I book you in relative to my kitchen fitter?
faqs.a: Before the units go in, ideally at first-fix stage, so sockets and circuits land in the right places once cabinets are fitted.

faqs.q: Can you work directly with my kitchen designer or fitter?
faqs.a: Yes, we regularly work from kitchen design plans and coordinate directly with the fitter.

faqs.q: Do induction hobs need a dedicated circuit?
faqs.a: Yes, almost always — we size the circuit to the specific hob you're installing.

faqs.q: Do you supply the sockets and lighting, or do I?
faqs.a: Either — we can supply everything, or fit fixtures and fittings you've already chosen.

faqs.q: How long does a kitchen electrical fit take?
faqs.a: First and second fix combined is typically 1–2 days, depending on the size of the kitchen and how much lighting is involved.

benefit: A Kitchen Wired Right, First Time

deepDive.heading: Planning Your Kitchen's Electrics Before the Units Go In
paragraphs: Kitchens carry more electrical load than any other room in the house, so getting the plan right before first fix matters more here than almost anywhere else. Ovens, hobs, and induction hobs in particular need their own dedicated circuit sized to that specific appliance — not shared with your general sockets — which is something we agree with you and your kitchen fitter before any cabinets go in.

Socket and lighting positions need to be locked in at first fix too, ideally before the units are ordered, since moving a socket after the cabinets are fitted means undoing tiling and worktops. Under-cabinet and feature lighting is easiest to wire in cleanly at this stage as well, so there's no visible cabling once everything's installed.

concerns.q: Do I need a full re-wire of the kitchen, or just new circuits for the appliances?
concerns.a: Usually just dedicated circuits for the new appliances and any layout changes — a full kitchen rewire is only needed if the existing wiring itself is old or undersized. We'll tell you honestly which applies once we've seen it.

concerns.q: Will the power be off while you work?
concerns.a: Only for the circuits we're connecting into, agreed around your kitchen fitter's schedule so it doesn't hold up the wider job.

concerns.q: How long will it take?
concerns.a: First and second fix combined is typically 1–2 days, depending on the size of the kitchen and how much lighting is involved.
